description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the marbled, gray-black stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, emphasizing the thickness and mass of the stone. The top surface appears slightly rough and matte, with subtle variations in color and texture typical of natural stone. The block is substantial in size, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment such as a sidewalk or plaza. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A large, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, made of marbled gray-black stone. The sides are solid and unmarked, giving it a heavy, durable appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, unmarked front face.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves on the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the grooved top and solid back face visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view, again emphasizing the parallel grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
